title: Kurukshetra — the Field of Dharma
subtitle: the Mahabharata war as a game of duty and choice

raja.name: Raja
raja.moral: Responsibility — the Self at the centre
raja.teaching: As the Raja, so the kingdom. Guard the centre of your being; when the Self falls, all is lost.

mantri.name: Mantri
mantri.teaching: The Mantri ranges the whole field, yet power without dharma is ruin. Wise counsel protects the Raja before it conquers.
mantri.fall: When the wise counsellor falls, the Raja stands alone against the storm.

ratha.name: Ratha
ratha.teaching: The chariot does not waver. Choose your line and move with force — momentum is built by not turning back.
ratha.fall: A broken chariot teaches that speed without protection ends in the dust.

gaja.name: Gaja
gaja.teaching: The Gaja treads its own diagonal with patience. True strength remembers its restraint and strikes only along its dharma.
gaja.fall: The great elephant falls, and the field learns that pride is heavier than tusks.

ashva.name: Ashva
ashva.teaching: When the straight path is blocked, the Ashva leaps. Think beyond the line; the horse alone crosses what others cannot.
ashva.fall: A fallen horse reminds us that cleverness, unguarded, is still mortal.

padati.name: Padati
padati.teaching: The Padati never steps backward. One square at a time, the humblest soldier does the work that wins the war.
padati.fall: A foot-soldier falls so the line may hold — no sacrifice on the field of dharma is small.

pawnTeachings: The foot-soldier never steps backward — in life, keep facing your dharma.
One square at a time, the humblest can reach the farthest rank.
Every Padati carries a Mantri's staff within: cross the whole field and be reborn greater.
Hold the line beside your brothers — a shield-wall outlasts any lone hero.
The first step is the hardest; after it, the march is simply duty.
Fear at the front is natural; courage is marching anyway.
You cannot un-take a step — weigh each advance, then commit fully.
A pawn guards more than it captures; steady presence is its own protection.
The sacrifice of the small can open the victory of the whole.
Act without clinging to the fruit — march, and let the field unfold as it will.
captureLines: On the field of dharma, even a righteous act may fell a kinsman — this was Arjuna's sorrow.
The victor mourns too; the wheel of karma turns for both hands.
A piece falls and the game advances — attachment is the only true loss.
Do your duty without hatred; the fallen also played their part in the whole.
Strike when dharma calls, but never with cruelty in the heart.
promotionLine: The steadfast Padati, having crossed the whole field of dharma, is reborn a Mantri. Perseverance is the quiet road to greatness.
checkLine: The Raja is threatened — gather your forces and protect the centre of your being.
checkmateLine: The war of Kurukshetra ends. Reflect, as Arjuna did: what was won, and at what cost?
